prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `902510d5`.
codex_response:
Implemented `902510d5` as a new ARC2 task package in [task_902510d5](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_902510d5). The rule is: keep the largest 8-connected nonzero object, remove the other singleton objects, use the singleton on the actual grid corner to choose the wedge corner, then draw a corner triangle whose color is the mode of the remaining singleton colors and whose side length is their count.

I used the Opus hint as the starting hypothesis and discarded the Sonnet hint for implementation; the correction is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_902510d5/notes.md). No new `arc2/dsl_ext.py` primitive was needed. The generator matches the task family by explicitly sampling a sparse diagonal-connected main object plus isolated corner/marker pixels, rather than deriving outputs from the verifier.
